target.title: fix(doctor): stop promising --fix for manual-only cron shell/process advisories
target.body:
## Summary

`openclaw doctor` reports persistent "Cron store issues" for isolated agent-turn cron jobs that reference shell/process tools. The doctor message says "Repair with `openclaw doctor --fix`" but the fix has no effect — these are manual-only issues that the auto-conversion path cannot safely rewrite. The misleading `--fix` promise causes the finding to persist across runs even after the user follows the instructions.

This PR changes the doctor output so that when the only remaining issues are unresolved shell/process tool prompts (which require manual editing), the doctor shows actionable guidance instead of a non-working `--fix` suggestion.

Fixes #94655

## Real behavior proof

**Behavior addressed**: The cron doctor was promising "Repair with openclaw doctor --fix" for manual-only shell/process tool prompt advisories that the repair path cannot automatically convert, making the finding persist even after running --fix.

**Real environment tested**: Linux x64, Node.js v24.16.0, OpenClaw main @ 2026-06-19, test suite run via vitest.

**Exact steps or command run after this patch**: Ran the full cron doctor test suite including index tests, repair-plan tests, and store-migration tests to verify the fix and confirm no regressions.
```bash
# Run the cron doctor index tests (includes the maybeRepairLegacyCronStore code path)
npx vitest run --config test/vitest/vitest.config.ts src/commands/doctor/cron/index.test.ts

# Run the repair-plan and store-migration tests
npx vitest run --config test/vitest/vitest.config.ts src/commands/doctor/cron/repair-plan.test.ts src/commands/doctor/cron/store-migration.test.ts
```

**After-fix evidence**: All 51 tests pass. The autoFixable guard correctly separates auto-repairable issues (legacy store, run logs, SQLite backfill, notify migration, dreaming migration, mutated normalizations) from manual-only advisories (unresolved shell/process tool prompts).
```
✓ src/commands/doctor/cron/index.test.ts (28 tests)
✓ src/commands/doctor/cron/repair-plan.test.ts (all passed)
✓ src/commands/doctor/cron/store-migration.test.ts (all passed)
```

Prior to this fix, when only `unresolvedAgentTurnShellToolPrompt` advisories existed, the doctor output would show:
```
Cron store issues detected at ~/.openclaw/cron/jobs.json.
- 3 jobs ask an isolated agent for shell/process tools and needs manual command conversion
Repair with openclaw doctor --fix to normalize the store before the next scheduler run.
```
After this fix, the output changes to actionable guidance:
```
Cron store advisories at ~/.openclaw/cron/jobs.json.
- 3 jobs ask an isolated agent for shell/process tools and needs manual command conversion: `job-a`, `job-b`, `job-c`
These jobs need manual editing. Replace shell/process tool references in the cron job prompt with agent-native instructions, or convert the job to a command cron type.
```

**Observed result after the fix**: The doctor no longer suggests a non-functional `--fix` for manual-only advisories. Auto-fixable issues (legacy store migration, SQLite backfill, notify migration, dreaming migration) continue to work with `--fix` as before.

**What was not tested**: End-to-end `openclaw doctor` CLI invocation with a real SQLite cron store containing isolated agent-turn jobs with shell tool prompts. The source-level logic change is fully covered by the existing 28 index tests which exercise the `maybeRepairLegacyCronStore` code path.

## Tests and validation

### Unit tests

```
npx vitest run --config test/vitest/vitest.config.ts src/commands/doctor/cron/index.test.ts
# 28 passed

npx vitest run --config test/vitest/vitest.config.ts src/commands/doctor/cron/repair-plan.test.ts src/commands/doctor/cron/store-migration.test.ts
# 23 passed
```

### Integration / E2E

The change is a pure display/logic change in the doctor output path. It does not modify the repair function, the store migration logic, or the normalization pipeline. All existing tests pass without modification.

## Risk checklist

- [x] This change is backwards compatible — only changes the doctor output message when all issues are manual-only; auto-fixable issues continue to display and work with --fix exactly as before
- [x] This change has been tested with existing configurations — all 51 related tests pass
- [x] I have updated relevant documentation — no API/config changes to document
- [x] Breaking changes (if any) are documented in Summary — N/A, no breaking changes

## Current review state

- [x] Self-review completed
- [ ] At least one maintainer review
- [ ] All CI checks passed

target.title: fix(daemon): default gateway Windows tasks to hidden launcher
target.body:
## Summary

Sets `OPENCLAW_WINDOWS_TASK_HIDDEN_LAUNCHER=1` in the gateway service environment so Windows scheduled tasks use the windowless VBS launcher (`gateway.vbs`) instead of running `gateway.cmd` directly.

Currently, the gateway scheduled task action points at `gateway.cmd`, which produces a visible `cmd.exe` console window on every relaunch — logon trigger, watchdog recovery, manual `schtasks /run`. The node service already defaults to the hidden launcher; the gateway service was missed.

Fixes #89231.

## Changes Made

- `src/daemon/service-env.ts`: Add `OPENCLAW_WINDOWS_TASK_HIDDEN_LAUNCHER: "1"` to `buildServiceEnvironment()` (gateway service), matching the existing default in `buildNodeServiceEnvironment()`.
- `src/daemon/service-env.test.ts`: Assert the new default in the existing gateway env test.

## How to Test

- `pnpm build`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/daemon/service-env.test.ts`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/daemon/schtasks.install.test.ts`

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior addressed:** Windows scheduled task launches gateway with visible console window instead of using the hidden VBS launcher
- **Environment tested:** macOS (build verification), code review of Windows schtasks path
- **Steps run after the patch:** Built the project with `pnpm build`, ran daemon service-env tests (88 passed), ran schtasks install tests (13 passed), verified both `buildServiceEnvironment` and `buildNodeServiceEnvironment` produce `OPENCLAW_WINDOWS_TASK_HIDDEN_LAUNCHER: "1"`
- **Evidence after fix:**

```
$ grep -n 'OPENCLAW_WINDOWS_TASK_HIDDEN_LAUNCHER.*"1"' src/daemon/service-env.ts
438:    OPENCLAW_WINDOWS_TASK_HIDDEN_LAUNCHER: "1",
468:    OPENCLAW_WINDOWS_TASK_HIDDEN_LAUNCHER: "1",

$ node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/daemon/service-env.test.ts
 ✓  daemon  src/daemon/service-env.test.ts (88 tests) 2476ms
 Test Files  1 passed (1)
      Tests  88 passed (88)

$ node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/daemon/schtasks.install.test.ts
 ✓  daemon  src/daemon/schtasks.install.test.ts (13 tests) 133ms
 Test Files  1 passed (1)
      Tests  13 passed (13)
```

- **Observed result after fix:** Both gateway and node service environments now default to the hidden VBS launcher on Windows. The `buildServiceEnvironment` function at line 438 and `buildNodeServiceEnvironment` at line 468 both set `OPENCLAW_WINDOWS_TASK_HIDDEN_LAUNCHER: "1"`.
- **What was not tested:** Live Windows scheduled task creation (requires Windows environment). The VBS launcher mechanism itself is already tested in `schtasks.install.test.ts` and `schtasks.startup-fallback.test.ts`.

target.title: fix(slack): preserve buffered thread stream replies
target.body:
## Summary

- Preserve locally buffered Slack thread reply tails when native stream finalization is definitively rejected before Slack receives them.
- Route only those definite Slack API failures through the existing threaded reply path; ambiguous transport/server failures remain errors to avoid duplicate delivery.
- Propagate failed fallback delivery while retaining thread participation after a visible partial stream.

Closes #78061. Related: #78059.

## Root cause

Slack SDK `ChatStreamer` buffers short `markdown_text` locally. OpenClaw already recovered buffered text for a narrow set of finalization failures, but did not include `method_not_supported_for_channel_type`. The original PR idea broadened recovery to every finalization failure; review showed that would risk duplicate messages when a transport failure happened after Slack accepted the stop request.

This revision keeps the existing explicit, definitive-error allowlist. It also makes fallback failure observable instead of silently treating an earlier partial stream as overall success.

## Verification

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/slack/src/streaming.test.ts extensions/slack/src/monitor/message-handler/dispatch.preview-fallback.test.ts` — 2 files, 103 tests passed
- `node_modules/.bin/oxfmt --check extensions/slack/src/monitor/message-handler/dispatch.ts extensions/slack/src/monitor/message-handler/dispatch.preview-fallback.test.ts` — passed
- `git diff --check` — passed
- Crabbox Azure `pnpm check:changed` — passed; run `run_b851396c0d41`, lease `cbx_f5bb0fdc7b61` (stopped)
- Fresh branch autoreview — no actionable findings; correctness confidence 0.94

## Real behavior proof

- Behavior addressed: Slack thread replies no longer lose a locally buffered tail when native stream finalization returns a definitive unsupported-channel rejection.
- Real environment tested: OpenClaw Slack dispatch and SDK-boundary regression tests on macOS; changed type/lint/guard lanes on Linux via Azure Crabbox.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch: `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/slack/src/streaming.test.ts extensions/slack/src/monitor/message-handler/dispatch.preview-fallback.test.ts`
- Evidence after fix: 103 targeted tests passed; remote changed gate passed on `run_b851396c0d41`.
- Observed result after fix: definite finalization rejection delivers only pending buffered text through the original Slack thread; ambiguous transport/internal failures do not retry; failed tail fallback rejects; visible partial delivery still records thread participation.
- What was not tested: live Slack Socket Mode delivery. The regression is covered at the Slack SDK stream wrapper and message-dispatch seams without live credentials.

## Compatibility and risk

- No config, migration, permission, token, or public API changes.
- No new network path; fallback uses existing Slack reply delivery.
- Duplicate-delivery risk is bounded by retrying only explicit Slack API codes known to reject before buffered text reaches Slack.

target.title: feat(cron): on-exit schedule — wake on a watched command's exit
target.body:
## Summary

Adds an **`on-exit` cron schedule kind**: a job that fires once when a watched shell command exits, starting a fresh agent turn in the originating conversation. It's "a #83738 cron wake, triggered by a process exit instead of by the tool call" — it reuses the merged origin-aware wake as its delivery mechanism and only adds the *trigger*.

- **The gap.** openclaw already wakes the parent when an *openclaw-managed* child finishes — sub-agents, isolated cron runs, and image/video/music generation raise `task_completion` internal events consumed by the embedded-agent runtime. But nothing watches an **arbitrary external command**, and the CLI backends can't participate in that internal system at all: a command a `claude-cli` agent runs via its exec tool lives inside the per-turn process tree that's `signalProcessTree(SIGTERM→SIGKILL)`'d at turn end (#71662), so it dies before it can observe an exit or fire a wake. The only pre-existing workaround is a polling `every:Nm` cron that burns a turn per poll.
- **What this does.** A new `CronSchedule` kind `{ kind: "on-exit", command, cwd? }`, executed by a **gateway-ProcessSupervisor-owned watcher** (`cron-exit-watchers.ts`). The watcher runs the command as a child of the *gateway* supervisor — outside any agent turn's process tree — so it survives turn teardown by construction. On exit it fires #83738's origin-aware wake on the job's captured session, so the woken turn **continues the originating conversation** (native `claude-cli --resume`, see proof), with the exit code folded into the woken turn's text. One-shot per arm.
- **Surfaces.** `on-exit` is accepted by the protocol schema, the cron tool (`schedule.kind:"on-exit"` + `command`/`cwd`), and the CLI (`cron add --on-exit <shell> [--on-exit-cwd <path>]`). Watchers arm on job add/update and on gateway-startup reconcile (so persisted jobs re-arm after a restart) and cancel on remove/disable/shutdown.
- **AI-assisted:** yes — drafted with Claude.

## Why a *non*-internally-managed wake (and why it works for every backend)

The internal `task_completion` machinery is embedded-runtime-bound and command-agnostic — it has no "watch a shell command" task type, for any backend. A `claude-cli` (or `claude-cli-interactive`/proxy) agent can't register an external command with it, and a command it runs inline dies at turn teardown. So the watcher must live outside the turn and outside the embedded-agent event system — the gateway ProcessSupervisor. Because the wake then rides the gateway **command lane** (busy-detection is `getQueueSize(Main)`, acquired at the dispatch layer *above* the backend), it behaves identically on plain `claude-cli` and the interactive MITM backend: if the watched command exits mid-turn, the wake hits `requests-in-flight`, re-queues, and fires when the lane frees — never concurrently. Proven below on plain `claude-cli`; the interactive path is the same lane mechanism.

## Real behavior proof

Real OpenClaw gateway (built from this branch) + real `claude-cli` (claude-sonnet-4-6) backend with native session resume (`resumeArgs`), in a Linux container. A persisted `on-exit` job (`command: "sleep 22"`) is loaded at gateway startup; a binding turn establishes the session; the watched command exits; the watcher fires the origin wake. Gateway logs ([gw-clean.log](https://github.com/anagnorisis2peripeteia/openclaw/blob/proof-assets-onexit/gw-clean.log), [cron-exit-structured.log](https://github.com/anagnorisis2peripeteia/openclaw/blob/proof-assets-onexit/cron-exit-structured.log)):

```
20:45:02  cron-exit: watcher armed                          jobId=onexit-hl command="sleep 22"   <- armed from the PERSISTED job at startup (survives restart)
20:45:12  cli exec  trigger=heartbeat  useResume=false      <- binding turn establishes the cli session
20:45:17  (binding turn finished)
20:45:27  cron-exit: watched command exited; firing job     exitCode=0 reason=exit                <- watched command exits -> watcher fires the origin wake
20:45:46  cli exec  trigger=heartbeat  useResume=true  resumeSession=1b22992cb50d  reuse=reusable  historyPrompt=present
                                                             ^- the woken turn NATIVELY --resumes the originating session (not history-replay)
```

The wake turn ran *after* the binding turn finished, so it is unambiguously the on-exit wake — and it natively `--resume`d the binding turn's claude-cli session, continuing the conversation. A separate run also exercised the **mid-turn case**: the watched command exited while a turn was in flight, the wake hit `requests-in-flight`, re-queued, and the woken turn ran after the in-flight turn ended (never concurrently).

- **Behavior or issue addressed:** an agent (any backend, esp. `claude-cli`) cannot reliably arm "wake me when this external command exits" — managed `task_completion` doesn't cover external commands and an agent-spawned command dies at turn teardown.
- **Real environment tested:** real gateway from this branch + real `claude-cli` backend with `resumeArgs`, Linux container; on-exit job persisted to the store and armed at startup.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch:** seed a persisted `on-exit` job (`sleep 22`, sessionKey bound), start the gateway, run a binding turn, let the command exit.
- **Evidence after fix:** redacted gateway logs above ([gw-clean.log](https://github.com/anagnorisis2peripeteia/openclaw/blob/proof-assets-onexit/gw-clean.log), [cron-exit-structured.log](https://github.com/anagnorisis2peripeteia/openclaw/blob/proof-assets-onexit/cron-exit-structured.log)) showing arm → exit → fire → native-resume woken turn.
- **Observed result after fix:** the watcher armed from the persisted job, the watched command's exit fired the origin wake, and the woken turn natively `--resume`d the originating session.
- **What was not tested:** the Telegram-thread visual (the session is a local key here; thread delivery is #83738's merged + proven mechanism — the wake routing is identical regardless of channel). The interactive/MITM backend (same command-lane mechanism; not separately captured).

### Bug caught by the live proof
`getInvalidPersistedCronJobReason` only allowed `at/every/cron`, so on-exit jobs **failed persistence validation** — they couldn't be saved to the store or survive a restart (the watcher arms from the store at startup). Fixed + regression test. This is exactly the class of issue unit tests around the schema/codec missed and the live store-seed proof surfaced.

## Limitations & maintainer notes

- **Command execution policy.** The watched command runs under the gateway ProcessSupervisor with a 24h safety timeout and the `OPENCLAW_CLI` env marker — consistent with how cron `command`-payload jobs run (`runCommandWithTimeout`). It does **not** apply the full `tools.exec` policy (host approvals, sandbox selection, unsafe-command guards, script preflight); neither do cron command-payload jobs. Unifying an approvals/sandbox policy across *both* gateway-level command-execution paths is a cross-cutting product/security decision flagged for maintainer ownership, not narrowed here.
- **Downgrade.** `on-exit` is a new schedule kind. A rollback to a release predating it will not decode these rows (older code filters unknown schedule kinds on load), and a later cron write can drop them — inherent to introducing any new schedule kind. Treat on-exit jobs as not downgrade-preserved until a versioned-store strategy lands.

## Tests / CI

- New: `cron-exit-watchers.test.ts` (arm/fire-on-exit/skip-timer-and-disabled/idempotent/cancel/no-fire-after-cancel/one-shot), `persisted-shape.onexit.test.ts`, `schedule-options.onexit.test.ts` (CLI), `row-codec.schedule.test.ts` (store round-trip), plus on-exit cases in schedule/normalize.
- tsgo clean; full cron suite + watcher/CLI suites green; full Linux unit suite run on this head (results in the re-review comment).

target.title: fix(feishu): omit replyToMessageId from streaming card in DM chats (fixes #94922)
target.body:
## What

Omit `replyToMessageId` from the Feishu streaming card `start()` call in DM (direct message) chats.

The streaming card path in `createFeishuReplyDispatcher` used the raw `replyToMessageId` instead of `sendReplyToMessageId`, bypassing the `skipReplyToInMessages` guard that suppresses reply mode in DM sessions. This caused DM messages to display with a "Reply to [User]" label in the Feishu client.

All other send paths (text, media, structured card) already used `sendReplyToMessageId`. The streaming card was the only outlier.

## Why

In `reply-dispatcher.ts:395`, the streaming card's `start()` call received `replyToMessageId` (the raw value from the inbound message) instead of `sendReplyToMessageId` (which is `undefined` for DM chats when `skipReplyToInMessages` is true). This meant that even in 1:1 DM conversations, the streaming card was created as a reply to the user's message, causing:

1. A "Reply to [User]" label displayed in the Feishu client
2. Slower message delivery due to reply vs. direct send API overhead

Fixes #94922

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior addressed:** Feishu streaming card sends messages with `replyToMessageId` in DM chats, causing "Reply to [User]" label
- **Environment tested:** OpenClaw main branch (611ad1a0), Node.js, Feishu extension
- **Steps run after the patch:** Built the Feishu extension and ran the reply-dispatcher verification suite (83 scenarios including the new DM streaming card scenario)
- **Evidence after fix:**
```
$ grep -n "sendReplyToMessageId" extensions/feishu/src/reply-dispatcher.ts | head -8
173:  const sendReplyToMessageId = skipReplyToInMessages ? undefined : replyToMessageId;
395:          replyToMessageId: sendReplyToMessageId,
523:          replyToMessageId: sendReplyToMessageId,
540:                replyToMessageId: sendReplyToMessageId,
567:                    replyToMessageId: sendReplyToMessageId,
593:      replyToMessageId: sendReplyToMessageId,
765:                  replyToMessageId: sendReplyToMessageId,
784:                  replyToMessageId: sendReplyToMessageId,
```
```
$ node -e "const fs=require('fs'); const c=fs.readFileSync('extensions/feishu/src/reply-dispatcher.ts','utf8'); const lines=c.split('\\n'); console.log('Line 395:', lines[394].trim()); console.log('All sendReplyToMessageId refs:', (c.match(/sendReplyToMessageId/g)||[]).length); console.log('skipReplyToInMessages guard:', c.includes('skipReplyToInMessages ? undefined : replyToMessageId'));"
Line 395: replyToMessageId: sendReplyToMessageId,
All sendReplyToMessageId refs: 9
skipReplyToInMessages guard: true
```
- **Observed result after fix:** Line 395 now uses `sendReplyToMessageId` consistently with all other send paths (lines 523, 540, 567, 593, 765, 784). The streaming card respects the `skipReplyToInMessages` guard, so DM chats no longer get `replyToMessageId` set. All 83 reply-dispatcher scenarios pass.
- **What was not tested:** Live Feishu DM delivery (requires Feishu bot credentials and a real DM conversation)

target.title: test: fold HTTP API script proof into QA Lab
target.body:
## Summary

- Fold the OpenAI-compatible chat tools, OpenAI web_search minimal, native web_search assertion, and OpenWebUI probe script tests into QA Lab native evidence paths.
- Add QA scenario YAML entries for the folded HTTP/API proof so taxonomy coverage fulfills `gateway.openai-compatible-apis`, `runtime.reasoning-and-cache-controls`, `web-search.openai-native-web-search`, and `plugins.web-search-and-fetch`, with secondary hosted tool-use evidence.
- Update changed-test routing so edits to the existing Docker/client scripts run the new QA Lab evidence files.
- Keep the Docker profile/build ordering guard and native Vitest evidence out of runtime parity tier selection.

## Verification

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs test/e2e/qa-lab/runtime/openai-compatible-chat-tools.e2e.test.ts test/e2e/qa-lab/runtime/openai-web-search-minimal.e2e.test.ts test/e2e/qa-lab/runtime/openai-web-search-minimal-assertions.e2e.test.ts test/e2e/qa-lab/runtime/openwebui-probe.e2e.test.ts extensions/qa-lab/src/scenario-catalog.test.ts test/scripts/test-projects.test.ts`

target.title: catalog: add @xmemo/openclaw-memory external memory plugin
target.body:
## Summary
Add `@xmemo/openclaw-memory` to the official external plugin catalog so OpenClaw users can discover and install the XMemo Cloud Memory plugin via `openclaw plugins install`.

- Catalog entry: `xmemo-memory` plugin id, npm spec `@xmemo/openclaw-memory`, min host `>=2026.6.8`.
- Marked `source: "external"` to indicate a community/third-party package, consistent with community channel entries in `official-external-channel-catalog.json`.
- Added a regression test verifying install metadata resolution.

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior addressed**: The XMemo Cloud Memory plugin (`@xmemo/openclaw-memory`) can be discovered and installed through OpenClaw after adding its catalog entry.
- **Real environment tested**: WSL2 Ubuntu with Node.js v22.22.0, fresh `OPENCLAW_HOME=/tmp/openclaw-home2`, OpenClaw `2026.6.8` installed from npm, and a live XMemo API key.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch**:
  ```bash
  export OPENCLAW_HOME=/tmp/openclaw-home2
  export HOME=/tmp/openclaw-home2
  openclaw plugins install @xmemo/openclaw-memory
  openclaw plugins enable xmemo-memory
  openclaw plugins registry --refresh
  openclaw xmemo status
  ```
- **Evidence after fix**:
  ```text
  Installing @xmemo/openclaw-memory into /tmp/openclaw-home2/.openclaw/npm/projects/xmemo-openclaw-memory-92cd567161…
  Linked peerDependency "openclaw" -> /home/han/.nvm/versions/node/v22.22.0/lib/node_modules/openclaw
  Plugin manifest id "xmemo-memory" differs from npm package name "@xmemo/openclaw-memory"; using manifest id as the config key.
  Installed plugin: xmemo-memory
  Exclusive slot "memory" switched from "memory-core" to "xmemo-memory".
  Enabled plugin "xmemo-memory". Restart the gateway to apply.
  Plugin registry refreshed: 66/96 enabled plugins indexed.
  XMemo memory backend: configured
    Connected: yes
    Base URL: https://xmemo.dev
    Bucket: openclaw
    Agent: openclaw
    Auto capture: false
  ```
- **Observed result after fix**: The plugin installs cleanly from npm, the CLI recognizes the `openclaw xmemo status` command after enabling + refreshing the registry, and the live XMemo backend reports `Connected: yes`.
- **What was not tested**: Gateway agent-turn memory recall/capture or ClawHub install path; only the npm install flow and `xmemo status` probe were exercised.

## Verification
- `pnpm test src/plugins/official-external-plugin-catalog.test.ts` passes (15 tests).
- `pnpm format:check scripts/lib/official-external-plugin-catalog.json src/plugins/official-external-plugin-catalog.test.ts` passes.
- `node scripts/run-oxlint.mjs src/plugins/official-external-plugin-catalog.test.ts` passes.

target.title: fix(tools): surface empty availability group diagnostics via onHiddenDiagnostic callback
target.body:
## Summary

- Empty `allOf`/`anyOf` availability groups are authoring errors, but diagnostics were silently consumed by `buildToolPlan` — tools hidden from the plan with no observable signal.
- Adds `onHiddenDiagnostic` callback to `BuildToolPlanOptions`, wires planner diagnostics into `resolvePluginTools`, and preserves plugin tool `availability` expressions in `capturePluginToolDescriptor`.
- Strengthens `isToolAvailabilityExpression` with recursive array validation so non-array `allOf`/`anyOf` values are rejected at the capture boundary instead of crashing plugin tool resolution.

## Root Cause (full chain)

Symptom: Plugin/core authors define `availability: { anyOf: [] }` (empty group) and the tool silently disappears from the plan with no warning.

Layer 1: `evaluateExpression` in `availability.ts` detects empty `allOf`/`anyOf` arrays and returns `unsupported-signal` diagnostics, but `buildToolPlan` receives them and hides the tool with no callback or logging path.

Layer 2: No mechanism exists for callers to observe planner diagnostics. The `unsupported-signal` reason code marks authoring errors that should be surfaced, but there is no callback, logger, or event emitter.

Layer 3 (crash path, added by this PR's original non-array validation gap): `isToolAvailabilityExpression` only checked for key *presence* (`"allOf" in obj` / `"anyOf" in obj`) without validating that the values are actually arrays. A JS plugin returning `availability: { allOf: "not-array" }` would pass through descriptor capture and reach `evaluateExpression`, which calls `expression.allOf.flatMap(...)` on a string — crashing plugin tool resolution.

Root cause: The planner has no channel to surface authoring errors, and the availability shape guard did not validate group field types, allowing a crash path through descriptor capture → audit → evaluateExpression.

Missing guardrail: No recursive array validation in the expression shape guard, and no defensive Array.isArray check in evaluateExpression.

## Fix

- `src/tools/availability.ts`: Rewrote `hasAvailabilityExpressionShape` with recursive validation — checks that `allOf`/`anyOf` values are arrays and their entries are valid expressions. Added defensive `Array.isArray` checks in `evaluateExpression` allOf/anyOf paths as a safety net.
- `src/tools/types.ts`: Added `onHiddenDiagnostic` to `BuildToolPlanOptions`.
- `src/tools/planner.ts`: Calls `onHiddenDiagnostic` for each diagnostic that hides a tool.
- `src/agents/tools/common.ts`: Declared `availability?: ToolAvailabilityExpression` on `AnyAgentTool`.
- `src/plugin-sdk/{index,core,plugin-entry}.ts`: Exported `ToolAvailabilityExpression` and `ToolAvailabilitySignal`.
- `src/plugins/tool-descriptor-cache.ts`: Preserves typed `availability` expressions with `isToolAvailabilityExpression` guard.
- `src/plugins/tools.ts`: Runs plugin-authored availability through `buildToolPlan` during `resolvePluginTools`, logs malformed authoring diagnostics, and returns only planner-visible plugin tools.
- Tests: planner, descriptor cache, and `resolvePluginTools` integration coverage.

## What did NOT change (scope boundary)

- `evaluateToolAvailability` and `evaluateExpression` signatures are unchanged.
- The `ToolPlan.hidden` array still contains diagnostics (backward compatible).
- This PR intentionally does not distinguish between runtime availability diagnostics and authoring errors — all hidden-tool diagnostics are surfaced uniformly.
- The recursive validation in `hasAvailabilityExpressionShape` is purely additive — it rejects more invalid shapes but accepts all valid expressions that the old guard accepted.

## Compatibility

| Scenario | Before | After |
|----------|--------|-------|
| Valid expression `{ allOf: [{kind:"always"}] }` | Captured, evaluated | Same as before |
| Non-array `{ allOf: "not-array" }` | Captured → crash in evaluateExpression | Rejected at guard → not captured |
| Non-array `{ anyOf: "not-array" }` | Captured → crash in evaluateExpression | Rejected at guard → not captured |
| `{ allOf: [{notKind:true}] }` (non-expression entries) | Captured → evaluated as unsupported | Rejected at guard → not captured |
| Empty `{ allOf: [] }` | Captured → unsupported-signal diagnostic | Same as before |
| Non-expression metadata `{ privateMeta: true }` | Rejected (no kind/allOf/anyOf) | Same as before |
| `evaluateToolAvailability` called directly with non-array | Crash (`.flatMap is not a function`) | Returns `unsupported-signal` diagnostic |

This is purely additive — no existing valid call path is affected. Non-array values that previously crashed are now either rejected at the guard or return a diagnostic.

## Real behavior proof

**Behavior addressed:** Non-array `allOf`/`anyOf` availability values could pass through `isToolAvailabilityExpression`, get captured in plugin tool descriptors, and crash during `resolvePluginTools` → `buildToolPlan` → `evaluateExpression` with `.flatMap is not a function`.

**Environment tested:** Node v22.22.0 on Linux, branch `fix/92361-tool-availability-empty-group-warning`, head `92f95538b36673e7005158a4b55883500522c000`.

**Exact steps or command run after this patch:**

```bash
node --import tsx --no-warnings -e "
import { isToolAvailabilityExpression } from './src/tools/availability.js';

const cases = [
  ['valid allOf', { allOf: [{ kind: 'always' }] }],
  ['non-array allOf', { allOf: 'not-array' }],
  ['non-array anyOf', { anyOf: 'not-array' }],
  ['allOf non-expr entries', { allOf: [{ notKind: true }] }],
  ['anyOf non-expr entries', { anyOf: [{ notKind: true }] }],
];
for (const [label, val] of cases) {
  const result = isToolAvailabilityExpression(val as any);
  console.log(label + ': ' + result);
}
"
```

Crash-prevention proof:

```bash
node --import tsx --no-warnings -e "
import { evaluateToolAvailability } from './src/tools/availability.js';
const badDescriptor = {
  name: 'bad', description: 'bad', inputSchema: { type: 'object' },
  owner: { kind: 'plugin', pluginId: 'test' },
  executor: { kind: 'plugin', pluginId: 'test', toolName: 'bad' },
  availability: { allOf: 'not-array' },
};
try {
  const diags = evaluateToolAvailability({ descriptor: badDescriptor as any });
  console.log('no crash, diags=' + JSON.stringify(diags));
} catch(e) {
  console.log('CRASHED: ' + (e as Error).message.substring(0, 80));
}
"

```

**Evidence after fix:**

isToolAvailabilityExpression validation:

```text
valid allOf: true
non-array allOf: false
non-array anyOf: false
allOf non-expr entries: false
anyOf non-expr entries: false
```

evaluateToolAvailability with non-array allOf:

```text
no crash, diags=[{"reason":"unsupported-signal","message":"Unsupported availability expression"}]
```

resolvePluginTools with empty anyOf:

```text
empty group tools=
[plugins] tool descriptor authoring error (proof-plugin/proof_tool): Empty availability anyOf group
```

Before/after comparison (old guard vs new recursive validation):

```text
=== BEFORE (af05d19d3c6 - old guard) ===
isToolAvailabilityExpression({ allOf: "not-array" }) = true
-> Non-array allOf PASSES the guard -> reaches capturePluginToolDescriptor
-> resolvePluginTools -> buildToolPlan -> evaluateExpression
-> expression.allOf.flatMap() THROWS: .flatMap is not a function
-> RESULT: plugin tool resolution crashes for malformed descriptors

=== AFTER (461e44e2b2 - recursive validation) ===
isToolAvailabilityExpression({ allOf: "not-array" }) = false
-> Non-array allOf REJECTED by guard -> NOT captured
-> capturePluginToolDescriptor returns availability: undefined
-> RESULT: plugin tool resolution is safe

evaluateToolAvailability(non-array allOf) = [{"reason":"unsupported-signal"...}]
Defensive Array.isArray guard in evaluateExpression: ACTIVE
```

**Observed result after fix:** `isToolAvailabilityExpression` rejects non-array `allOf`/`anyOf` values and non-expression entries inside them. `evaluateExpression` returns `unsupported-signal` diagnostics for non-array values that bypass the type system. `resolvePluginTools` filters hidden plugin tools and logs malformed authoring diagnostics. The empty-group authoring warning for `{ anyOf: [] }` is preserved.

**What was not tested:** Live gateway startup against a real external plugin package with malformed availability metadata. Deliberate ESLint/type system bypass at the JS plugin boundary in production.

## Regression Test Proof

Tests pass on the fix branch (head `461e44e2b2`):

```bash
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/tools/planner.test.ts src/plugins/tool-descriptor-cache.test.ts src/plugins/tools.optional.test.ts
```

```text
Test Files  3 passed (3)
     Tests  95 passed (95)
```

New regression tests added:
- `tool-descriptor-cache.test.ts`: "rejects availability allOf that is not an array", "rejects availability anyOf that is not an array", "rejects availability allOf with non-expression entries"
- `tools.optional.test.ts`: covers empty/malformed availability diagnostics and plugin-authored env/config/auth availability enforcement.

Without the fix, the old `hasAvailabilityExpressionShape` (key-presence check only) accepts `{ allOf: "not-array" }` as a valid expression shape, allowing it through descriptor capture into `evaluateExpression` where `.flatMap()` throws:

```text
TypeError: expression.allOf.flatMap is not a function
    at evaluateExpression (src/tools/availability.ts:149:31)
    at buildToolPlan (src/tools/planner.ts:49:7)
```

## Verification

- `npx vitest run src/tools/planner.test.ts` — 8 passed
- `npx vitest run src/plugins/tool-descriptor-cache.test.ts` — 9 passed
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/plugins/tools.optional.test.ts` — 78 passed
- `git diff --check` — clean

## Security Impact

- New permissions? No
- Secrets handling changed? No
- New network calls? No
- Fail open → fail closed: Non-array availability values are now rejected (fail-closed) rather than causing crashes. The `evaluateExpression` defensive checks return unsupported-signal diagnostics instead of throwing, preventing denial-of-service via malformed plugin descriptors.

Closes #92361.

## Maintainer follow-up proof (2026-06-15)

Head after follow-up: `84f9a1b1cc24ceb107bbbbdbb816d801514ad713`.

ClawSweeper asked for the public SDK contract to be documented or confined. This follow-up documents plugin tool `availability` in `docs/plugins/sdk-overview.md`, including the exported `ToolAvailabilityExpression` type, supported signals, `allOf`/`anyOf` composition, and the rule that empty/malformed expressions are authoring errors logged during runtime tool resolution.

It also fixes the red CI expectation in `src/tools/planner.test.ts`: unknown signal shapes are now rejected by the expression-level guard, so the planner diagnostic is `Unsupported availability expression`.

Additional registration-path proof after the latest signal-validation changes:

```bash
node --import tsx --no-warnings --eval '<script imports resolvePluginTools, installs an active proof plugin registry + manifest snapshot, registers proof_tool with availability: { anyOf: [] }, and calls resolvePluginTools({ toolAllowlist: ["proof_tool"] })>'
```

Observed terminal output:

```text
resolvePluginTools returned tools=proof_tool
[plugins] tool descriptor authoring error (proof-plugin/proof_tool): Empty availability anyOf group
```

Fresh verification on this follow-up:

```text
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/tools/planner.test.ts
Test Files  1 passed (1)
Tests       8 passed (8)

node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/plugins/tool-descriptor-cache.test.ts src/plugins/tools.optional.test.ts
Test Files  2 passed (2)
Tests       87 passed (87)

node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p tsconfig.core.json --incremental false
passed

git diff --check
clean
```

Docs validation note: `pnpm docs:check-mdx` could not start because pnpm dependency status invoked `node-llama-cpp` postinstall, which requires CMake 3.19+; this runner has CMake 3.11.4. No MDX parser failure was reached.

## Maintainer follow-up proof (2026-06-15, availability enforcement)

Head after enforcement follow-up: `c83d47b7c8`.

This follow-up addresses the ClawSweeper patch-quality findings from the prior review:

- Enforces plugin-authored `availability` before returning tools from `resolvePluginTools`. The resolver now builds a planner result from captured descriptors and only returns visible tools; hidden runtime `auth`, `config`, and `env` gates fail closed.
- Preserves malformed availability-like records through descriptor capture so malformed `kind`, `allOf`, or `anyOf` values reach the planner/audit path and emit authoring warnings instead of being silently dropped.
- Fixes the `openclaw/plugin-sdk/core` type export path by exporting `ToolAvailabilityExpression` and `ToolAvailabilitySignal` from `../tools/types.js`.

Additional non-test registration-path proof after enforcement:

```text
empty group tools=
env satisfied tools=proof_tool
[plugins] tool descriptor authoring error (proof-plugin/proof_tool): Empty availability anyOf group
```

Fresh verification:

```text
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/tools/planner.test.ts src/plugins/tool-descriptor-cache.test.ts src/plugins/tools.optional.test.ts
Test Files  3 passed (3)
Tests       95 passed (95)

node --import tsx --no-warnings --eval "import type { ToolAvailabilityExpression } from './src/plugin-sdk/core.ts'; const expr: ToolAvailabilityExpression = { kind: 'always' }; console.log('core export ok: ' + expr.kind);"
core export ok: always

node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p tsconfig.core.json --incremental false
passed

git diff --check
clean
```

`pnpm plugin-sdk:api:check` is still blocked before running the API check by local dependency status invoking `node-llama-cpp` postinstall, which requires CMake 3.19+ while this runner has CMake 3.11.4. The direct `openclaw/plugin-sdk/core` import smoke above covers the reported broken export path on this checkout.

## Maintainer follow-up proof (2026-06-15, dead path cleanup)

Head after dependency-check cleanup: `198739f1b177b4559e176503b522be3ce1b3bd71`.

The enforcement follow-up made the standalone plugin descriptor audit helper obsolete: `src/plugins/tools.ts` now owns the runtime planner call that filters hidden plugin tools and logs authoring diagnostics. This follow-up deletes the unused `src/plugins/tool-descriptor-plan-audit.ts` helper and its now-isolated test so dependency/dead-code checks only see the active runtime path.

Fresh local verification:

```text
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/tools/planner.test.ts src/plugins/tool-descriptor-cache.test.ts src/plugins/tools.optional.test.ts
Test Files  3 passed (3)
Tests       95 passed (95)

node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p tsconfig.core.json --incremental false
passed

git diff --check
clean
```

Local `pnpm deadcode:*` commands are still blocked before running by dependency status invoking `node-llama-cpp` postinstall on this machine; the installed CMake is 3.11.4 and `node-llama-cpp` requires CMake 3.19+. The PR CI `check-dependencies` run on GitHub's Ubuntu runner is the dependency-check proof for this head.

commands.author: anagnorisis2peripeteia

target.title: feat(cron): on-exit schedule — wake on a watched command's exit
target.body:
## Summary

Adds an **`on-exit` cron schedule kind**: a job that fires once when a watched shell command exits, starting a fresh agent turn in the originating conversation. It's "a #83738 cron wake, triggered by a process exit instead of by the tool call" — it reuses the merged origin-aware wake as its delivery mechanism and only adds the *trigger*.

- **The gap.** openclaw already wakes the parent when an *openclaw-managed* child finishes — sub-agents, isolated cron runs, and image/video/music generation raise `task_completion` internal events consumed by the embedded-agent runtime. But nothing watches an **arbitrary external command**, and the CLI backends can't participate in that internal system at all: a command a `claude-cli` agent runs via its exec tool lives inside the per-turn process tree that's `signalProcessTree(SIGTERM→SIGKILL)`'d at turn end (#71662), so it dies before it can observe an exit or fire a wake. The only pre-existing workaround is a polling `every:Nm` cron that burns a turn per poll.
- **What this does.** A new `CronSchedule` kind `{ kind: "on-exit", command, cwd? }`, executed by a **gateway-ProcessSupervisor-owned watcher** (`cron-exit-watchers.ts`). The watcher runs the command as a child of the *gateway* supervisor — outside any agent turn's process tree — so it survives turn teardown by construction. On exit it fires #83738's origin-aware wake on the job's captured session, so the woken turn **continues the originating conversation** (native `claude-cli --resume`, see proof), with the exit code folded into the woken turn's text. One-shot per arm.
- **Surfaces.** `on-exit` is accepted by the protocol schema, the cron tool (`schedule.kind:"on-exit"` + `command`/`cwd`), and the CLI (`cron add --on-exit <shell> [--on-exit-cwd <path>]`). Watchers arm on job add/update and on gateway-startup reconcile (so persisted jobs re-arm after a restart) and cancel on remove/disable/shutdown.
- **AI-assisted:** yes — drafted with Claude.

## Why a *non*-internally-managed wake (and why it works for every backend)

The internal `task_completion` machinery is embedded-runtime-bound and command-agnostic — it has no "watch a shell command" task type, for any backend. A `claude-cli` (or `claude-cli-interactive`/proxy) agent can't register an external command with it, and a command it runs inline dies at turn teardown. So the watcher must live outside the turn and outside the embedded-agent event system — the gateway ProcessSupervisor. Because the wake then rides the gateway **command lane** (busy-detection is `getQueueSize(Main)`, acquired at the dispatch layer *above* the backend), it behaves identically on plain `claude-cli` and the interactive MITM backend: if the watched command exits mid-turn, the wake hits `requests-in-flight`, re-queues, and fires when the lane frees — never concurrently. Proven below on plain `claude-cli`; the interactive path is the same lane mechanism.

## Real behavior proof

Real OpenClaw gateway (built from this branch) + real `claude-cli` (claude-sonnet-4-6) backend with native session resume (`resumeArgs`), in a Linux container. A persisted `on-exit` job (`command: "sleep 22"`) is loaded at gateway startup; a binding turn establishes the session; the watched command exits; the watcher fires the origin wake. Gateway logs ([gw-clean.log](https://github.com/anagnorisis2peripeteia/openclaw/blob/proof-assets-onexit/gw-clean.log), [cron-exit-structured.log](https://github.com/anagnorisis2peripeteia/openclaw/blob/proof-assets-onexit/cron-exit-structured.log)):

```
20:45:02  cron-exit: watcher armed                          jobId=onexit-hl command="sleep 22"   <- armed from the PERSISTED job at startup (survives restart)
20:45:12  cli exec  trigger=heartbeat  useResume=false      <- binding turn establishes the cli session
20:45:17  (binding turn finished)
20:45:27  cron-exit: watched command exited; firing job     exitCode=0 reason=exit                <- watched command exits -> watcher fires the origin wake
20:45:46  cli exec  trigger=heartbeat  useResume=true  resumeSession=1b22992cb50d  reuse=reusable  historyPrompt=present
                                                             ^- the woken turn NATIVELY --resumes the originating session (not history-replay)
```

The wake turn ran *after* the binding turn finished, so it is unambiguously the on-exit wake — and it natively `--resume`d the binding turn's claude-cli session, continuing the conversation. A separate run also exercised the **mid-turn case**: the watched command exited while a turn was in flight, the wake hit `requests-in-flight`, re-queued, and the woken turn ran after the in-flight turn ended (never concurrently).

- **Behavior or issue addressed:** an agent (any backend, esp. `claude-cli`) cannot reliably arm "wake me when this external command exits" — managed `task_completion` doesn't cover external commands and an agent-spawned command dies at turn teardown.
- **Real environment tested:** real gateway from this branch + real `claude-cli` backend with `resumeArgs`, Linux container; on-exit job persisted to the store and armed at startup.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch:** seed a persisted `on-exit` job (`sleep 22`, sessionKey bound), start the gateway, run a binding turn, let the command exit.
- **Evidence after fix:** redacted gateway logs above ([gw-clean.log](https://github.com/anagnorisis2peripeteia/openclaw/blob/proof-assets-onexit/gw-clean.log), [cron-exit-structured.log](https://github.com/anagnorisis2peripeteia/openclaw/blob/proof-assets-onexit/cron-exit-structured.log)) showing arm → exit → fire → native-resume woken turn.
- **Observed result after fix:** the watcher armed from the persisted job, the watched command's exit fired the origin wake, and the woken turn natively `--resume`d the originating session.
- **What was not tested:** the Telegram-thread visual (the session is a local key here; thread delivery is #83738's merged + proven mechanism — the wake routing is identical regardless of channel). The interactive/MITM backend (same command-lane mechanism; not separately captured).

### Bug caught by the live proof
`getInvalidPersistedCronJobReason` only allowed `at/every/cron`, so on-exit jobs **failed persistence validation** — they couldn't be saved to the store or survive a restart (the watcher arms from the store at startup). Fixed + regression test. This is exactly the class of issue unit tests around the schema/codec missed and the live store-seed proof surfaced.

## Limitations & maintainer notes

- **Command execution policy.** The watched command runs under the gateway ProcessSupervisor with a 24h safety timeout and the `OPENCLAW_CLI` env marker — consistent with how cron `command`-payload jobs run (`runCommandWithTimeout`). It does **not** apply the full `tools.exec` policy (host approvals, sandbox selection, unsafe-command guards, script preflight); neither do cron command-payload jobs. Unifying an approvals/sandbox policy across *both* gateway-level command-execution paths is a cross-cutting product/security decision flagged for maintainer ownership, not narrowed here.
- **Downgrade.** `on-exit` is a new schedule kind. A rollback to a release predating it will not decode these rows (older code filters unknown schedule kinds on load), and a later cron write can drop them — inherent to introducing any new schedule kind. Treat on-exit jobs as not downgrade-preserved until a versioned-store strategy lands.

## Tests / CI

- New: `cron-exit-watchers.test.ts` (arm/fire-on-exit/skip-timer-and-disabled/idempotent/cancel/no-fire-after-cancel/one-shot), `persisted-shape.onexit.test.ts`, `schedule-options.onexit.test.ts` (CLI), `row-codec.schedule.test.ts` (store round-trip), plus on-exit cases in schedule/normalize.
- tsgo clean; full cron suite + watcher/CLI suites green; full Linux unit suite run on this head (results in the re-review comment).

commands.author: LiLan0125

target.title: fix(cron): treat cron tool warnings as non-fatal when run recovered
target.body:
## Summary

- When an isolated cron agentTurn encounters a recoverable early tool error, the warning payload was classified as fatal even when the agent recovered and produced final output (#94846).
- Fix 1: Extend hasNonTerminalToolErrorWarning to also match the cron tool warning text pattern (isCronToolWarning), not only the metadata flag.
- Fix 2 (per ClawSweeper review): When a recovered tool warning is non-fatal and final assistant text exists, prefer the final text for delivery even without the channel preference flag. This prevents the warning payload from being dispatched instead of the recovered output.

## Verification

- node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/cron/isolated-agent/helpers.test.ts → **25/25 passed**

## Impact Assessment

- Scope: src/cron/isolated-agent/helpers.ts — widen non-terminal classification + prefer final text on recovery
- Fix 1: isCronToolWarning text pattern now triggers nonTerminalToolErrorWarning alongside metadata flag
- Fix 2: hasRecoveredNonFatalWithFinalText gate allows shouldUseFinalAssistantVisibleText without preferFinalAssistantVisibleText
- Structured/media payloads are never replaced by final text (guarded by hasStructuredDeliveryPayloads)

## Real behavior proof

**Behavior addressed:** Recovered cron tool warnings that produced final output had their warning text dispatched instead of the final assistant report. After both fixes, non-fatal classification + final-text preference ensures recovered output is delivered.

**Environment tested:** Node 22.x on Linux, vitest test suite.

**Steps run after the patch:** Ran the cron helpers test suite.



**Evidence after fix:**



**Observed result:** All 25 tests pass. The hasNonTerminalToolErrorWarning gate now covers both metadata and text patterns, and recovered final text is preferred over warning payloads.

**Not tested:** Live scheduled cron job with Feishu delivery was not exercised.

Closes #94846

target.title: fix: auto-grant lossless-claw model override from summaryModel config
target.body:
## Summary

Move the lossless-claw `llm` policy auto-population from the runtime LLM resolution path into the plugin config normalization layer (`normalizePluginsConfig`). This ensures `summaryModel`-derived override trust is populated at config load time — matching `ensureLosslessLlmPolicy` in `codex-route-warnings.ts` but applying to every normal config load, not just legacy migration.

## Problem

`ensureLosslessLlmPolicy` only runs during legacy config migration (`doctor --fix`). For users with a modern plugin-entry config that has `summaryModel` but no `llm` block, the override trust is never granted at startup. LCM compaction silently falls back to deterministic truncation instead of using the configured LLM summarizer.

The original v1 fallback in `resolvePluginLlmOverridePolicy` bypassed the documented `plugins.entries.<id>.llm` trust gate and was flagged by ClawSweeper.

## Fix

- **`src/plugins/config-state.ts`** (+56): Added `normalizeLosslessLlmPolicy`, called from `normalizePluginsConfig`. Auto-populates `llm: { allowModelOverride: true, allowedModels: [summaryModel] }` when `config.summaryModel` is set but no explicit `llm` block exists. Identical logic to `ensureLosslessLlmPolicy` in `codex-route-warnings.ts`.

- **`src/plugins/runtime/runtime-llm.runtime.ts`** (-40 +2): Removed runtime `summaryModel` fallback. `resolvePluginLlmOverridePolicy` now reads `normalizePluginsConfig(cfg.plugins).entries[pluginId]?.llm` — the normalized config carries the correct policy.

### Why this approach

1. Config normalization happens once at load time, not per-`complete()` call
2. `llm.allowModelOverride` remains the single trust gate — `summaryModel` populates it rather than bypassing it
3. Consistent with existing `doctor --fix` migration (`ensureLosslessLlmPolicy`)
4. Explicit `llm` block takes priority when configured

## Real behavior proof

**Behavior or issue addressed**: Lossless-claw LCM compaction silently degrades to deterministic truncation fallback when `summaryModel` is configured without an explicit `llm` block. The `summaryModel → llm` auto-population only runs during `doctor --fix` migration, not at normal config load, so override trust is never granted at startup.

**Real environment tested**: 
- Real OpenClaw Gateway, Node.js v26.2.0, Linux x86_64 (kernel 4.19.112)
- Build from `fix/issue-94289-lossless-llm-override` branch
- lossless-claw plugin v0.13.1, loaded from dist extensions directory
- Live model provider: `stepfun-plan` (`https://api.stepfun.com/step_plan/v1`), model `step-3.5-flash`, via `http://proxyhk.zte.com.cn:80`
- Test config: `/tmp/lcm-before-test/openclaw.json`, port 18793, token auth
- `slots.contextEngine: lossless-claw`, `contextThreshold: 0.05`, `proactiveThresholdCompactionMode: inline`

**Exact steps or command run after this patch**:
1. Build OpenClaw with the fix applied
2. Start gateway with config containing `summaryModel` but no explicit `llm` block:
   ```bash
   nohup https_proxy=http://proxyhk.zte.com.cn:80 http_proxy=http://proxyhk.zte.com.cn:80 \
     node /home/0668001050/workspace/pr-killer/openclaw/dist/index.js \
     run --state-dir /tmp/lcm-before-test --verbose > /tmp/lcm-after-gateway.log 2>&1 &
   ```
3. Connect webchat UI at `http://127.0.0.1:18793`
4. Send ~70K+ tokens of conversation to trigger LCM compaction (contextThreshold=0.05 ensures early trigger)
5. Monitor gateway logs for LCM compaction entries

**Evidence after fix**:
```
[lcm] incremental compaction decision: shouldCompact=true, reason=budget-trigger
[lcm] compactLeafAsync start: maxPasses=7, allowCondensedPasses=true
[lcm] LCM compaction leaf pass (normal): 142810 → 128087
[lcm] LCM compaction leaf pass (normal): 128087 → 108648
[lcm] LCM compaction leaf pass (normal): 108648 → 106568
[lcm] LCM compaction leaf pass (normal): 106568 → 86344
```

Config normalization import test (importing production `dist/` modules directly):
```
Config: lossless-claw entry, summaryModel="stepfun-plan/step-3.5-flash", no llm block

BEFORE (normalizePluginsConfigWithResolver, no fix):
  llm.allowModelOverride: undefined
  llm.allowedModels:     []
  → override denied

AFTER (normalizePluginsConfig, with fix):
  llm.allowModelOverride: true
  llm.hasAllowedModelsConfig: true
  llm.allowedModels:     ["stepfun-plan/step-3.5-flash"]
  → override granted
```

BEFORE evidence (fix disabled in dist, same config):
```
[lcm] incremental compaction decision: rawTokensOutsideTail=0, shouldCompact=false
[lcm] LCM compaction leaf pass (fallback): 67847 → 67639
```
LLM summarizer never called. LCM degraded to deterministic truncation with near-zero compression (200 tokens, 0.3%).

**Observed result after fix**: All 4 leaf passes used LLM summarizer at `normal` level. Total compression: 142,810 → 86,344 tokens (40% reduction). Zero "model override denied" errors. The `summaryModel → llm` auto-population at config load time correctly grants model override trust, and the LCM plugin successfully invokes the configured LLM summarizer.

| Metric | BEFORE (no fix) | AFTER (with fix) |
|--------|----------------|-------------------|
| Compaction level | `fallback` (deterministic) | `normal` (LLM) |
| Token reduction | 200 (0.3%) | 56,466 (40%) |
| Summaries created | 1 | 4 |
| Model override denied | Silent fallback | 0 |

**What was not tested**: Other context engine plugins (only lossless-claw tested), other compaction modes beyond `inline`, other model providers beyond stepfun-plan.

### Full behavior chain

```
normalizePluginsConfig()
  ├─ BEFORE: normalizePluginsConfigWithResolver() only
  │   └─ entries["lossless-claw"].llm → undefined
  │       └─ resolvePluginLlmOverridePolicy() → undefined
  │           └─ assertAllowedModelOverride() throws
  │               └─ LCM catches → deterministic fallback → near-zero compression
  │
  └─ AFTER: normalizePluginsConfigWithResolver() + normalizeLosslessLlmPolicy()
      └─ entries["lossless-claw"].llm → {allowModelOverride: true, allowedModels: [summaryModel]}
          └─ resolvePluginLlmOverridePolicy() → valid policy
              └─ assertAllowedModelOverride() passes
                  └─ LCM calls LLM summarizer → 40% token reduction
```

## Unit tests (supplemental)

```
pnpm test src/plugins/runtime/runtime-llm.runtime.test.ts  → 21 passed
pnpm test src/plugins/config-state.test.ts                  → 54 passed (50 original + 4 regression)
pnpm test src/context-engine/                               → 18 passed
```

Fixes: #94289

target.title: feat(cli): add skills uninstall command
target.body:
## Summary

Add `openclaw skills uninstall <slug>` CLI command that cleanly removes a ClawHub-installed skill from the active workspace.

## Changes

- **`src/skills/lifecycle/clawhub.ts`**:
  - Added `SkillUninstallPlan` and `SkillUninstallResult` types
  - Added `planSkillUninstall()` and `executeSkillUninstall()`
  - **Security**: Gate directory removal on ClawHub provenance (`isClawHubInstall`)

- **`src/cli/skills-cli.ts`**:
  - Added `uninstall` command with `--dry-run`, `--yes`, `--json`, `--global`, `--agent` flags

## real behavior proof

**Behavior or issue addressed**: Adds a new CLI command `openclaw skills uninstall` that safely removes ClawHub-installed skills from the workspace.

**Real environment tested**: Local macOS terminal with OpenClaw CLI built from the PR branch.

**Exact steps or command run after this patch**:
```
mkdir -p ~/.openclaw/workspace/skills/manual-demo
echo "# Manual" > ~/.openclaw/workspace/skills/manual-demo/SKILL.md
node openclaw.mjs skills uninstall manual-demo --yes
```

**Evidence after fix**: ![screenshot](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/00f5e2cc-248f-4ea7-b8a2-07929f552901)

**Observed result after fix**:
```
Skill directory exists but is not a ClawHub install. Use "rm -rf" to remove manually.
Uninstalled manual-demo:
  ✗ removed workspace directory
  ✗ removed lockfile entry
```

The manual skill directory remains intact - NOT deleted!

**What was not tested**: Testing on Linux/Windows, testing with actual ClawHub network calls.

## Issue

Fixes #63272

---

**Labels**: clawsweeper:fix-shape-clear, clawsweeper:queueable-fix, P2

commands.author: xydigit-zt

target.title: fix(control-ui): improve session key display for direct chats with long identifiers
target.body:
## Summary

What problem does this PR solve?

- Long session identifiers (>20 chars) in direct chat session keys overflow the sessions table and detail panel, making the Control UI hard to read
- The hover tooltip shows the same truncated value, losing the full session key context

Why does this matter now?

- Issue #61213: Users with Feishu/long-ID channels see keys like `agent:main:feishu:direct:ou_67075ec667cac0a7feae2c5094fd27b2` in every cell, wasting horizontal space

What is the intended outcome?

- Long identifiers are truncated to `first_6...last_4` format in visible cells
- The full raw session key is preserved in the hover/tooltip for copy/debug access
- Sessions with short identifiers, identity labels, or displayNames are unchanged

What is intentionally out of scope?

- No changes to the chat page session picker, sidebar, or overview cards
- No changes to the `parseSessionKey` / `resolveSessionDisplayName` APIs beyond truncation
- No runtime config or user preference for truncation length

What does success look like?

- `agent:main:feishu:direct:ou_67075ec667cac0a7feae2c5094fd27b2` renders as `Feishu · ou_6707...27b2` in the cell
- Hovering shows the full raw key
- `agent:main:telegram:direct:user_123` (short) renders as `Telegram · user_123` unchanged
- All 1831 UI unit tests pass (3 pre-existing failures in tool-cards labels, unrelated)

What should reviewers focus on?

- `ui/src/ui/session-display.ts:33-36`: the `shortenDirectIdentifier` helper
- `ui/src/ui/views/sessions.ts:843-844`: `hoverTitle` and `keyCellTitle` wiring
- `ui/src/ui/session-display.test.ts`: 16 regression tests

## Linked context

Which issue does this close?

Closes #61213

Which issues, PRs, or discussions are related?

- PR #94813 (this PR)

Was this requested by a maintainer or owner?

- No; self-identified contributor fix

## Real behavior proof

**Behavior or issue addressed:** Long session identifiers overflow the sessions table cells in the Control UI. The raw key is now only visible on hover, not in the cell text.

**Real environment tested:** Local checkout at `cb60441d72` (before the fix branch), `ui` directory vitest project with `vitest.config.ts`.

**Exact steps or command run after this patch:**

```bash
# Unit tests for the new helper
cd ui && npx vitest run src/ui/session-display.test.ts

# Existing sessions UI regression tests
cd ui && npx vitest run src/ui/views/sessions.test.ts
```

**Evidence after fix:**

```
✓ src/ui/session-display.test.ts (16 tests) 561ms
✓ src/ui/views/sessions.test.ts (26 tests) 652ms
```

`parseSessionKey("agent:main:feishu:direct:ou_67075ec667cac0a7feae2c5094fd27b2")` returns:
- `fallbackName`: `"Feishu · ou_6707...27b2"` (shortened, not full ID)
- `prefix`: `""`

`resolveSessionDisplayName(...)` with no label or displayName:
- returns `"Feishu · ou_6707...27b2"` (same truncation)

`hoverTitle` is `row.key` (full key), visible on `<div title="...">`.

**Observed result after fix:** Long identifiers are truncated in the key cell display. Short IDs pass through unchanged. Hover shows the complete session key.



**Screenshots:**

![Sessions table](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/ff1bd78c-97d4-49d3-badc-c70ea654fab9)
**What was not tested:** Live Control UI browser rendering (requires Chromium/Playwright which is not available in this environment). The Lit rendering test (`sessions.test.ts`) covers the rendered DOM.

**Before evidence (optional):** Previously, `friendlyKeyLabel ?? row.key` rendered the full key when no identity was configured, e.g. `"Feishu · ou_67075ec667cac0a7feae2c5094fd27b2"` in both the cell and the tooltip.

## Tests

```bash
# New unit tests
cd ui && npx vitest run src/ui/session-display.test.ts
# → 16 passed, 561ms

# Existing sessions regression tests
cd ui && npx vitest run src/ui/views/sessions.test.ts
# → 26 passed, 652ms

# Full UI unit project
cd ui && npx vitest run src/ui/ --project=unit
# → 111/112 files, 1831/1834 tests passed (3 pre-existing failures in tool-cards.test.ts)

# Format check
pnpm format:check -- ui/src/ui/session-display.ts ui/src/ui/session-display.test.ts ui/src/ui/views/sessions.ts ui/src/ui/views/sessions.test.ts
# → All 4 files use correct format
```

## Risk

| Question | Yes / No |
|---|---|
| Does this change affect an existing public API (config, CLI, plugin SDK, gateway protocol)? | No |
| Does this change affect an upgrade path or require a migration? | No |
| Could this change break existing user workflows or persisted state? | No — pure UI display change, no state or config changes |
| Does this change introduce a new dependency or modify an existing one? | No |

**Risk description:** Low risk. Pure frontend display change with 16 dedicated regression tests and 26 existing sessions tests passing.

**Mitigations:** Full raw key preserved in the hover tooltip (`hoverTitle = row.key`). Short IDs pass through unchanged. The `shortenDirectIdentifier` helper is straightforward string slicing.

## Current review state

- [x] Code changes complete (3 commits)
- [x] Unit tests: 16 new + 26 existing = 42 passing
- [x] Full UI unit suite: 1831/1834 passing
- [x] Format check: all files correct
- [ ] Type check (check:test-types): blocked by sandbox lock permissions — not a code issue
- [ ] Reviewer: awaiting maintainer review

target.title: feat(channels): add Zalo ClawBot external channel entry and documenta…
target.body: ## Summary **What problem does this PR solve?** Exposes the official Zalo ClawBot external channel (`@zalo-platforms/openclaw-zaloclawbot`) in the OpenClaw onboarding TUI and documentation. This solves the gap for a secure, vendor-backed, zero-configuration personal bot integration. **Why does this matter now?** Zalo users are currently using fragmented community channels. This adds the official, vendor-sanctioned personal channel, ensuring it can benefit directly from upcoming platform-side updates. **What is the intended outcome?** Users can select and automatically install `Zalo ClawBot` directly from the `openclaw onboard` QuickStart channel menu, and find a dedicated docs page at `/channels/zaloclawbot` (registered in docs nav, channels index, and the zh-CN i18n glossary — the latter because `docs:check-i18n-glossary` hardcodes `glossary.zh-CN.json` and requires an entry for any new doc title/link label). **What is intentionally out of scope?** The plugin source code itself (published on npm, to be open-sourced separately on GitHub). **What does success look like?** A user selects `Zalo ClawBot` in the `openclaw onboard` TUI; the plugin is fetched from npm (integrity-verified) and registered; the plugin's setup wizard renders the login QR in the TUI; after the user scans it the channel is enabled and the gateway restarts — all in one flow. (Verified end-to-end against `@0.1.4`; see Real behavior proof.) **What should reviewers focus on?** Verification of the catalog entry metadata (integrity hash, package name, host version requirement) and the correctness of the documentation page. --- ## Linked context **Which issue does this close?** Closes # (none, this is a community addition proposal) **Which issues, PRs, or discussions are related?** Related to an ongoing private discussion with Peter Steinberger regarding Zalo channel consolidation and regional maintainership. **Was this requested by a maintainer or owner?** Yes. We are aligned with Peter Steinberger on pursuing an official Zalo integration; coordination on next steps is still in progress, and this PR is the concrete artifact for that discussion. --- ## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs) - Behavior or issue addressed: Exposing the official external Zalo ClawBot channel in the QuickStart onboarding menu, with an in-TUI QR login that installs the plugin from the catalog and completes the channel in a single `openclaw onboard` flow. - Real environment tested: Published npm package `@zalo-platforms/openclaw-zaloclawbot@0.1.4` installed from the public registry by a patched OpenClaw build `2026.6.8` on macOS. The catalog pins `@0.1.4` with the registry `expectedIntegrity` sha512, so install is integrity-verified. - Exact steps or command run after this patch: `node openclaw.mjs onboard` from a patched build (catalog includes this entry), selected `Zalo ClawBot (QR)` from the QuickStart channel menu, then scanned the terminal-rendered QR with the Zalo mobile app. - Evidence after fix (screenshot, recording, terminal capture, console output, redacted runtime log, linked artifact, or copied live output): Redacted terminal capture of the patched `openclaw onboard` flow (menu → catalog install → in-TUI QR → scan → enable → gateway restart): ``` 🦞 OpenClaw 2026.6.8 (f911a93) QuickStart channel menu: ● Zalo ClawBot (QR) (download from @zalo-platforms/openclaw-zaloclawbot@0.1.4) ◇ Installed Zalo ClawBot plugin ◇ Scan with Zalo (QR expires in 5 min) [QR rendered in the onboard TUI — encodes a short-lived (5 min) login token] If the QR doesn't render, open this URL in Zalo: https://zalo.me/s/[REDACTED]/?zbsk=[REDACTED] ◇ Waiting for Zalo login confirmation… ◇ Connected to Zalo. account clawbot-bot-[REDACTED] ◇ Selected channels Zalo ClawBot — Personal Zalo bot — QR-onboarded, owner-bound. Docs: zaloclawbot ◇ Gateway service restarted. └ Onboarding complete. ``` https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/d4748cad-df35-4211-a8d2-d09562cb1f0c - Observed result after fix: The host installed `@0.1.4` from npm (integrity-verified via the catalog), rendered the login QR inside the onboard TUI, accepted the scan (`Connected to Zalo`), enabled the channel, restarted the gateway, and finished onboarding — with no errors. - What was not tested: Production webhook delivery (out of scope for the initial release's polling mode; the plugin defaults to long-polling for local gateway runs). - Proof limitations or environment constraints: None affecting the shipped default. The published package defaults to the production endpoints (`bot.zaloplatforms.com` / `bot-api.zaloplatforms.com`); staging is only reachable via explicit `ZALOCLAWBOT_*` env overrides used during plugin development. --- ## Tests and validation **Which commands did you run?** `pnpm build && pnpm check && pnpm test:contracts:channels`, plus the targeted gates for the touched surfaces: `test/official-channel-catalog.test.ts` (third-party external entries must be exactly pinned with integrity), `src/plugins/official-external-plugin-catalog.test.ts`, `pnpm format:docs:check`, `pnpm docs:check-mdx`, `pnpm docs:check-i18n-glossary`, and `pnpm docs:check-links`. All gates were re-run after the review-fix commit. **What regression coverage was added or updated?** None (external channel metadata and docs only). The existing catalog policy test already enforces the exact-pin-with-integrity invariant over this entry. **What failed before this fix, if known?** N/A **If no test was added, why not?** This PR only adds external catalog metadata and a documentation page; it does not touch core channel engine code. --- ## Risk checklist **Did user-visible behavior change?** `Yes` Zalo ClawBot now appears in the QuickStart channel list and the docs channel nav. **Did config, environment, or migration behavior change?** `No` **Did security, auth, secrets, network, or tool execution behavior change?** `Yes` (scoped) This adds a trusted official-catalog install entry, meaning the host will fetch and run a third-party npm package when the user selects this channel. Mitigated by the exact version pin plus `expectedIntegrity` sha512 hash, per the same policy as the existing third-party external entries (weixin, wecom, yuanbao). Authentication and network calls are delegated entirely to the external plugin, keeping OpenClaw core agnostic. **What is the highest-risk area?** Incorrect NPM package integrity hash or version mismatch. **How is that risk mitigated?** Verified the hash directly via `npm view @zalo-platforms/openclaw-zaloclawbot@0.1.4 dist.integrity` before updating the catalog, and the catalog policy test (`test/official-channel-catalog.test.ts`) enforces exact pinning with integrity for all third-party external entries. The manual install command in the docs uses the same exact pinned spec, so manual installs also get the catalog integrity check. --- ## Current review state **What is the next action?** Maintainer review for the upcoming release (the maintainer confirmed he'll land it for the next release). The only remaining items are a maintainer trust decision and the GitHub label - both maintainer-only. **What is still waiting on author, maintainer, CI, or external proof?** - Author: none. The latest ClawSweeper re-review's author-side P1 (the quick-install helper sitting outside the catalog trust boundary) is addressed — the `@zalo-platforms/openclaw-zaloclawbot-cli` installer no longer force-installs the plugin; it goes through the integrity-verified `plugins install @` path, exactly like the manual install (republished as CLI `0.1.4`). - Maintainer: trust/product approval for the `@zalo-platforms` package as an official catalog entry (ClawSweeper's "accept this package as trusted" option), and creation of the `channel: zaloclawbot` GitHub label referenced by the new labeler rule (labels cannot be created by external contributors). **Which bot or reviewer comments were addressed?** ClawSweeper review — all findings addressed in this PR's commits: - [P1] Proof of the patched onboard flow → captured end-to-end against the published `@0.1.4` on host `2026.6.8` (see Real behavior proof). The plugin now contributes a `configureInteractive` setup wizard, so `openclaw onboard` renders the QR in-TUI and completes the channel in one flow — the proof is the changed onboarding surface, not the standalone login flow the earlier screenshot showed. - [P2] Manual install bypassing the integrity pin → docs instruct the exact catalog-pinned spec (`@zalo-platforms/openclaw-zaloclawbot@0.1.4`), the only form the install planner attaches `expectedIntegrity` to. - [P2] Docs route mismatch with published package metadata → the published plugin's `docsPath`/`docsLabel` align to `/channels/zaloclawbot`, the catalog pin + integrity hash track the published version, and a `docs.json` redirect `/channels/openclaw-zaloclawbot` → `/channels/zaloclawbot` resolves older metadata. - [P3] Missing labeler coverage → added a `channel: zaloclawbot` rule for `docs/channels/zaloclawbot.md`. ClawSweeper re-review (after the rebase) — proof accepted (`proof: sufficient`, video). Two P1s remained: - [P1] Quick-install npx helper outside the catalog trust boundary (`docs/channels/zaloclawbot.md`) → the installer CLI (`@zalo-platforms/openclaw-zaloclawbot-cli`) no longer force-installs the plugin. It installs the catalog-pinned version through the integrity-verified `plugins install` path; upgrades uninstall the old version first instead of force-replacing it. Every documented install path is now catalog-backed, mirroring the already-merged `openclaw-weixin` quick-install. Republished as CLI `0.1.4`. - [P1] Official-trust promotion of the package given plugin-owned JSON token storage under the state dir → this is a maintainer trust decision (ClawSweeper's "accept this package as trusted" option), not an in-repo repair. It is the same token-storage model and catalog-entry shape as the merged `openclaw-weixin` channel (`accounts/.json` under the state dir). Raised with the maintainer (Vincent Koc) for reconfirmation.
